name: Popover
description: Display popover section relative to given target element

markdown: # Popover Package: @mantine/core Import: import { Popover } from '@mantine/core'; Description: Display popover section relative to given target element ## Usage ```tsx import { Popover, Text, Button } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( This is uncontrolled popover, it is opened when button is clicked ); } ``` ## Controlled You can control the Popover state with the `opened` and `onChange` props: ```tsx import { useState } from 'react'; import { Button, Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { const [opened, setOpened] = useState(false); return ( Dropdown ); } ``` Controlled example with mouse events: ```tsx import { useDisclosure } from '@mantine/hooks'; import { Popover, Text, Button } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { const [opened, { close, open }] = useDisclosure(false); return ( This popover is shown when user hovers the target element ); } ``` ## Focus trap If you need to use interactive elements (inputs, buttons, etc.) inside `Popover.Dropdown`, set the `trapFocus` prop: ```tsx import { Popover, Button, TextInput } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( ); } ``` ## Inline elements Enable the `inline` middleware to use `Popover` with inline elements: ```tsx import { Popover, Mark, Text } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Stantler’s magnificent antlers were traded at high prices as works of art. As a result, this Pokémon was hunted close to extinction by those who were after the priceless antlers.{' '} When visiting a junkyard Inline dropdown , you may catch sight of it having an intense fight with Murkrow over shiny objects.Ho-Oh’s feathers glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light. These feathers are said to bring happiness to the bearers. This Pokémon is said to live at the foot of a rainbow. ); } ``` ## Same width Set the `width="target"` prop to make the Popover dropdown take the same width as the target element: ```tsx import { Popover, Text, Button } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( This popover has same width as target, it is useful when you are building input dropdowns ); } ``` ## offset Set the `offset` prop to a number to change the dropdown position relative to the target element. This way you can control the dropdown offset on the main axis only. ```tsx import { Popover, Button, Text } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Change position and offset to configure dropdown offset relative to target ); } ``` To control offset on both axes, pass an object with `mainAxis` and `crossAxis` properties: ```tsx import { Popover, Button, Text } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Change position and offset to configure dropdown offset relative to target ); } ``` ## Middlewares You can enable or disable [Floating UI](https://floating-ui.com/) middlewares with the `middlewares` prop: * [shift](https://floating-ui.com/docs/shift) middleware shifts the dropdown to keep it in view. It is enabled by default. * [flip](https://floating-ui.com/docs/flip) middleware changes the placement of the dropdown to keep it in view. It is enabled by default. * [inline](https://floating-ui.com/docs/inline) middleware improves positioning for inline reference elements that span over multiple lines. It is disabled by default. * [size](https://floating-ui.com/docs/size) middleware manipulates the dropdown size. It is disabled by default. Example of turning off `shift` and `flip` middlewares: ```tsx import { Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( {/* Popover content */} ); } ``` ## Customize middleware options To customize [Floating UI](https://floating-ui.com/) middleware options, pass them as an object to the `middlewares` prop. For example, to change the [shift](https://floating-ui.com/docs/shift) middleware padding to `20px`, use the following configuration: ```tsx import { Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( {/* Popover content */} ); } ``` ## Dropdown arrow Set the `withArrow` prop to add an arrow to the dropdown. The arrow is a `div` element rotated with `transform: rotate(45deg)`. The `arrowPosition` prop determines how the arrow is positioned relative to the target element when `position` is set to `*-start` and `*-end` values on the `Popover` component. By default, the value is `center` – the arrow is positioned in the center of the target element if it is possible. If you change `arrowPosition` to `side`, then the arrow will be positioned on the side of the target element, and you will be able to control the arrow offset with the `arrowOffset` prop. Note that when `arrowPosition` is set to `center`, the `arrowOffset` prop is ignored. ```tsx import { Popover, Button, Text } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Arrow position can be changed for *-start and *-end positions ); } ``` ## With overlay Set the `withOverlay` prop to add an overlay behind the dropdown. You can pass additional configuration to the [Overlay](https://mantine.dev/llms/core-overlay.md) component with the `overlayProps` prop: ```tsx import { Popover, Avatar, Text, Group, Anchor, Stack } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Mantine @mantinedev Customizable React components and hooks library with focus on usability, accessibility and developer experience 0 Following 1,174 Followers ); } ``` ## Hide detached Use the `hideDetached` prop to configure how the dropdown behaves when the target element is hidden with styles (`display: none`, `visibility: hidden`, etc.), removed from the DOM, or when the target element is scrolled out of the viewport. By default, `hideDetached` is enabled – the dropdown is hidden with the target element. You can change this behavior with `hideDetached={false}`. To see the difference, try to scroll the root element of the following demo: ```tsx import { Box, Button, Group, Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( This popover dropdown is hidden when detached This popover dropdown is visible when detached ); } ``` ## Disabled Set the `disabled` prop to prevent `Popover.Dropdown` from rendering: ```tsx import { Popover, Text, Button } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Disabled popover dropdown is always hidden ); } ``` ## Click outside By default, `Popover` closes when you click outside of the dropdown. To disable this behavior, set `closeOnClickOutside={false}`. You can configure events that are used for click-outside detection with the `clickOutsideEvents` prop. By default, `Popover` listens to `mousedown` and `touchstart` events. You can change it to any other events, for example, `mouseup` and `touchend`: ```tsx import { Popover, Text, Button } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( Popover will be closed with mouseup and touchend events ); } ``` ## onDismiss If you need to control the opened state, but still want to close the popover on outside clicks and escape key presses, use the `onDismiss` prop: ```tsx import { useState } from 'react'; import { Button, Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { const [opened, setOpened] = useState(false); return ( setOpened(false)} > Dropdown ); } ``` ## Initial focus Popover uses the [FocusTrap](https://mantine.dev/llms/core-focus-trap.md) component to manage focus. Add the `data-autofocus` attribute to the element that should receive initial focus: ```tsx import { Popover } from '@mantine/core'; function Demo() { return ( ); } ``` ## Target component The target element determines where the Popover will be positioned relative to. ## Nested popovers Nested popovers require children rendering without [Portal](https://mantine.dev/llms/core-portal.md). Usually, you should disable the portal with props of the component that renders popover content. For example, [Select](https://mantine.dev/llms/core-select.md) has a `comboboxProps={{ withinPortal: false }}` prop. Check the documentation of the component that you are using to render popover content to find out how to disable the portal. If the portal is not disabled, outside clicks will close all popovers. Example of disabling the portal in [Select](https://mantine.dev/llms/core-select.md) and [DatePickerInput](https://mantine.dev/llms/dates-date-picker-input.md) components: ```tsx import { Button, Popover, Select } from '@mantine/core'; import { DatePickerInput } from '@mantine/dates'; function Demo() { return (

props.name: arrowOffset
props.description: Arrow offset in px

props.name: arrowPosition
props.description: Arrow position

props.name: arrowRadius
props.description: Arrow border-radius in px

props.name: arrowSize
props.description: Arrow size in px

props.name: children
props.description: Popover.Target and Popover.Dropdown components

props.name: clickOutsideEvents
props.description: Events that trigger outside clicks

props.name: closeOnClickOutside
props.description: Determines whether dropdown should be closed on outside clicks

props.name: closeOnEscape
props.description: Determines whether dropdown should be closed when Escape key is pressed

props.name: defaultOpened
props.description: Initial opened state for uncontrolled component

props.name: disabled
props.description: If set, popover dropdown will not be rendered

props.name: floatingStrategy
props.description: Changes floating ui [position strategy](https://floating-ui.com/docs/usefloating#strategy)

props.name: hideDetached
props.description: If set, the dropdown is hidden when the element is hidden with styles or not visible on the screen

props.name: id
props.description: Id base to create accessibility connections

props.name: keepMounted
props.description: If set, the dropdown is not unmounted from the DOM when hidden. display: none styles are added instead.

props.name: middlewares
props.description: Floating ui middlewares to configure position handling
props.defaultValue: { flip: true, shift: true, inline: false }

props.name: offset
props.description: Offset of the dropdown element

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called with current state when dropdown opens or closes

props.name: onClose
props.description: Called when dropdown closes

props.name: onDismiss
props.description: Called when the popover is dismissed by clicking outside or by pressing escape

props.name: onEnterTransitionEnd
props.description: Called when enter transition ends

props.name: onExitTransitionEnd
props.description: Called when exit transition ends

props.name: onOpen
props.description: Called when dropdown opens

props.name: onPositionChange
props.description: Called when dropdown position changes

props.name: opened
props.description: Controlled dropdown opened state

props.name: overlayProps
props.description: Props passed down to Overlay component

props.name: portalProps
props.description: Props to pass down to the Portal when withinPortal is true

props.name: position
props.description: Dropdown position relative to the target element

props.name: preventPositionChangeWhenVisible
props.description: Prevents popover from flipping/shifting when it the dropdown is visible

props.name: radius
props.description: Key of theme.radius or any valid CSS value to set border-radius

props.name: returnFocus
props.description: Determines whether focus should be automatically returned to control when dropdown closes

props.name: shadow
props.description: Key of theme.shadows or any other valid CSS box-shadow value

props.name: transitionProps
props.description: Props passed down to the Transition component. Use to configure duration and animation type.

props.name: trapFocus
props.description: Determines whether focus should be trapped within dropdown

props.name: width
props.description: Dropdown width, or 'target' to make dropdown width the same as target element

props.name: withArrow
props.description: Determines whether component should have an arrow

props.name: withOverlay
props.description: Determines whether the overlay should be displayed when the dropdown is opened

props.name: withRoles
props.description: Determines whether dropdown and target elements should have accessible roles

props.name: withinPortal
props.description: Determines whether dropdown should be rendered within the Portal

props.name: zIndex
props.description: Dropdown z-index

props.name: children
props.description: Target element

props.name: popupType
props.description: Popup accessible type

props.name: refProp
props.description: Key of the prop that should be used to access element ref
